title: NurbsSurface Object
description: Transient NURBS surface. A transient NURBS surface is not displayed or saved in a document. A transient NURBS surface is used as a wrapper to work with raw NURBS surface information. A transient NURBS surface is bounded by it's natural boundaries and does not support the definition of arbitrary boundaries. A NURBS surface is typically obtained from a BREPFace object, which does have boundary information. They are created statically using the create method of the NurbsSurface class.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: copy
methods.description: Creates and returns an independent copy of this NurbsSurface object.

methods.name: create
methods.description: Creates a transient NURBS surface object.

methods.name: getData
methods.description: Gets the data that defines the NURBS surface.

methods.name: set
methods.description: Sets the data that defines the NURBS surface.

methods.name: transformBy
methods.description: Updates this surface by transforming it with a given input matrix.

properties.name: controlPointCountU
properties.description: Gets the number of control points in the U direction.

properties.name: controlPointCountV
properties.description: Gets the number of control points in the V direction.

properties.name: controlPoints
properties.description: Gets an array of control points from the surface.

properties.name: degreeU
properties.description: Gets the degree in the U direction.

properties.name: degreeV
properties.description: Gets the degree in the V direction.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: knotCountU
properties.description: Gets the knot count in the U direction.

properties.name: knotCountV
properties.description: Gets thekKnot count in the V direction.

properties.name: knotsU
properties.description: Get the knot vector from the U direction.

properties.name: knotsV
properties.description: Get the knot vector from the V direction

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: propertiesU
properties.description: Gets the properties (NurbsSurfaceProperties) of the surface in the U direction.

properties.name: propertiesV
properties.description: Gets the properties (NurbsSurfaceProperties) of the surface in the V direction.

classType.title: NurbsSurface.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

syntax.python: returnValue = adsk.core.NurbsSurface.classType()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = adsk::core::NurbsSurface::classType();

classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

copy.title: NurbsSurface.copy Method
copy.description: Creates and returns an independent copy of this NurbsSurface object.

syntax.python: returnValue = nurbsSurface_var.copy()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = nurbsSurface_var->copy();

copy.return_description: Returns a new NurbsSurface object that is a copy of this NurbsSurface object.

create.title: NurbsSurface.create Method
create.description: Creates a transient NURBS surface object.

syntax.python: returnValue = adsk.core.NurbsSurface.create(degreeU, degreeV, controlPointCountU, controlPointCountV, controlPoints, knotsU, knotsV, weights, propertiesU, propertiesV)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = adsk::core::NurbsSurface::create(degreeU, degreeV, controlPointCountU, controlPointCountV, controlPoints, knotsU, knotsV, weights, propertiesU, propertiesV);

create.return_description: Returns the new NurbsSurface object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: degreeU
parameters.description: The degree in the U direction.

parameters.name: degreeV
parameters.description: The degree in the V direction.

parameters.name: controlPointCountU
parameters.description: The number of control points in the U direction.

parameters.name: controlPointCountV
parameters.description: The number of control points in the V direction.

parameters.name: controlPoints
parameters.description: An array of surface control points. The length of this array must be controlPointCountU * controlPointCountV.

parameters.name: knotsU
parameters.description: The knot vector for the U direction.

parameters.name: knotsV
parameters.description: The knot vector for the V direction.

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description: An array of weights that corresponds to the control points of the surface.

parameters.name: propertiesU
parameters.description: The properties (NurbsSurfaceProperties) of the surface in the U direction.

parameters.name: propertiesV
parameters.description: The properties (NurbsSurfaceProperties) of the surface in the V direction.

getData.title: NurbsSurface.getData Method
getData.description: Gets the data that defines the NURBS surface.

syntax.python: (returnValue, degreeU, degreeV, controlPointCountU, controlPointCountV, controlPoints, knotsU, knotsV, weights, propertiesU, propertiesV) = nurbsSurface_var.getData()
syntax.cpp: #include getData(degreeU, degreeV, controlPointCountU, controlPointCountV, controlPoints, knotsU, knotsV, weights, propertiesU, propertiesV);

parameters.name: degreeV

parameters.name: knotsU

parameters.name: knotsV
parameters.description: The output knot vector for the V direction.

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description: An output array of weights that corresponds to the control points of the surface.

parameters.name: propertiesU
parameters.description: The output properties (NurbsSurfaceProperties) of the surface in the U direction.

parameters.name: propertiesV
parameters.description: The output properties (NurbsSurfaceProperties) of the surface in the V direction.

transformBy.title: NurbsSurface.transformBy Method
transformBy.description: Updates this surface by transforming it with a given input matrix.

syntax.python: returnValue = nurbsSurface_var.transformBy(matrix)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = nurbsSurface_var->transformBy(matrix);

transformBy.return_description: Returns true if the transform was successful.

parameters.name: matrix
parameters.description: A 3D matrix that defines the transform to apply to the surface.
